appearance defect.alcohol: Using the alcohol and applicator provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.alcohol + rag: Using the alcohol and rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.alcohol wipes: Using the alcohol wipes provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.buffer pad: Using the buffer pad provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.deburring tool: Using a Deburring Tool, apply light to moderate pressure to the appearance defect and roll the deburring tool away from you. Repeat until the appearance defect is removed.
appearance defect.dremel rotary tool: Using the dremel rotary tool provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.dry rag: Using the dry rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.file: Using your file, apply a light to moderate pressure to the appearance defect until it is removed from the suspect area.
appearance defect.foam brush: Using the foam brush provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.hand: Using your hand, firmly grasp the appearance defect and lift it gently away from the surface of the suspect area.
appearance defect.hand tap: Using a Hand Tap, apply light to moderate pressure in a clockwise direction to cut threads. While turning clockwise, it is crucial to reverse the tap (turn counterclockwise) periodically—roughly every half to full turn—to break metal chips, prevent jamming, and avoid breaking the tap inside the hole
appearance defect.hammer/chisel: Hold the chisel in your non-dominant hand, and place it flush against the surface while applying pressure to the base of the appearance defect. Using your dominant hand, strike the head (butt) of the chisel with the hammer. Repeat this as needed to remove the weld splatter, but be sure not to dig the corners of the chisel into the surface, causing damage.
appearance defect.hook pick: Using a Hook Pick, apply light to moderate pressure to lift away the appearance defect.
appearance defect.magic eraser: Using the magic eraser provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.microfiber towel: Using the microfiber towel provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.polish cloth: Using the polish cloth provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.q-tips: Using the Q-Tips provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.rag: Using the rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the appearance defect has been removed.
appearance defect.round file: Using around file, apply moderate pressure to the appearance defect and make a back and forth motion, until the appearance defect is removed.
appearance defect.scissors: Using your scissors, gently cut away the appearance defect from the suspect area.
appearance defect.snips: Using your snips, gently cut away the appearance defect from the suspect area.
appearance defect.steel file: Using your file, apply a light to moderate pressure to the appearance defect, ina back and forth motion, until it is removed from the suspect area.
appearance defect.tweezers: Using a Hand Tap, apply light to moderate pressure to lift away the appearance defect.

rust.dry rag: Using the dry rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the rust has been removed.
rust.file: Using your file, apply a light to moderate pressure to the rust, in a back and forth motion, until it is removed from the suspect area.
rust.microfiber towel: Using the microfiber towel provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the rust has been removed.
rust.rag: Using the rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the rust has been removed.
rust.round file: Using a round file, apply moderate pressure to the rust and make a back and forth motion, until the rust is removed.

thread contamination.alcohol: Using the alcohol and applicator provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.alcohol + rag: Using the alcohol and rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.alcohol wipes: Using the alcohol wipes provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.buffer pad: Using the buffer pad provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.drill with tap: Using a drill with the appropriate tap, apply light pressure to the drill's trigger in the forward ( or clockwise) direction to cut threads. While turning clockwise, it is crucial to reverse the tap (turn counterclockwise) periodically—roughly every half to full turn—to break metal chips, prevent jamming, and avoid breaking the tap inside the hole
thread contamination.dry rag: Using the dry rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.hand tap: Using a Hand Tap, apply light to moderate pressure in a clockwise direction to cut threads. While turning clockwise, it is crucial to reverse the tap (turn counterclockwise) periodically—roughly every half to full turn—to break metal chips, prevent jamming, and avoid breaking the tap inside the hole
thread contamination.hook pick: Using a hook pick, apply light to moderate pressure to lift away the thread contamination.
thread contamination.microfiber towel: Using the microfiber towel provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.polish cloth: Using the polish cloth provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.q-tips: Using the Q-Tips provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.rag: Using the rag provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.white gas: Using the white gas and applicator provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.white gas + white pad: Using the white gas and white pad provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.white pad: Using the white pad provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.
thread contamination.wire brush: Using the wire brush provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the thread contamination has been removed.

thread issue.drill with tap: Using a drill with the appropriate tap, apply light pressure to the drill's trigger in the forward ( or clockwise) direction to cut threads. While turning clockwise, it is crucial to reverse the tap (turn counterclockwise) periodically—roughly every half to full turn—to break metal chips, prevent jamming, and avoid breaking the tap inside the hole

weld splatter.deburring tool: Using a Deburring Tool, apply light to moderate pressure to the weld splatter and roll the deburring tool away from you. Repeat until the weld splatter is removed.
weld splatter.dremel rotary tool: Using the dremel rotary tool provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the weld splatter has been removed.
weld splatter.drill with tap: Using a drill with the appropriate tap, apply light pressure to the drill's trigger in the forward ( or clockwise) direction to cut threads. While turning clockwise, it is crucial to reverse the tap (turn counterclockwise) periodically—roughly every half to full turn—to break metal chips, prevent jamming, and avoid breaking the tap inside the hole
weld splatter.hand tap: Using a Hand Tap, apply light to moderate pressure in a clockwise direction to cut threads. While turning clockwise, it is crucial to reverse the tap (turn counterclockwise) periodically—roughly every half to full turn—to break metal chips, prevent jamming, and avoid breaking the tap inside the hole
weld splatter.hammer/chisel: Hold the chisel in your non-dominant hand, and place it flush against the surface while applying pressure to the base of the weld splatter. Using your dominant hand, strike the head (butt) of the chisel with the hammer. Repeat this as needed to remove the weld splatter, but be sure not to dig the corners of the chisel into the surface, causing damage.
weld splatter.round file: Using around file, apply moderate pressure to the weld splatter and make a back and forth motion, until the weld splatter is removed.
weld splatter.steel file: Using your steel file, apply a light to moderate pressure to the weld splatter, in a back and forth motion, until it is removed from the suspect area.
weld splatter.wire brush: Using the wire brush provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the weld splatter has been removed.

workability / tight.dremel rotary tool: Using the dremel rotary tool provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until the material causing the rough operation has been removed.
workability / tight.hand: Using your hands, work the part back and forth to loosen the rough operation. Repeat as needed.
workability / tight.hand pull: Using your hands, work the part back and forth to loosen the rough operation. Repeat as needed.
workability / tight.wire brush: Using the wire brush provided, apply a light to moderate pressure in small circles until material causing the rough operation has been removed.

ppeRules.Default: Rubber palm gloves, bump cap, orange safety vest, safety glasses, and safety shoes are required at all times.
ppeRules.Body Weld: Kevlar gloves and Kevlar sleeves are required at all times in Body Weld.
ppeRules.Engine Line + Lineside: Hard hat, Kevlar gloves, and Kevlar sleeves are required. Standard PPE including orange safety vest, safety glasses, and safety shoes must be worn at all times.
ppeRules.Chassis + Online: Hard hat, Kevlar gloves, and Kevlar sleeves are required. Standard PPE including orange safety vest, safety glasses, and safety shoes must be worn at all times.
ppeRules.Finger + Burr / Weld Splatter + Metal: Use the fingers of your cut resistant glove to inspect the suspect area.

dataRules.Online (Line) + Trim 1-2 / Final 1-3 / W: Sequence sheet required in addition to the data sheet.
dataRules.Online (Line) + Paint / Body Weld: Body sequence sheet required in addition to the data sheet.
dataRules.Area or Lineside: Tally sheet required in addition to the data sheet.

focalOverrides.Finger + Burr / Weld Splatter + Metal: Use 'using the fingers of your cut resistant glove' instead of standard finger language.
focalOverrides.Gauge: Step 2 instructs inspector to use a gauge to measure. Step 3/4 reference the measurable criteria.
